childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

node.textContent: Generate the PRD markdown and review
cycle 0 · draft attempt 0 · clarify rounds 1 · judge human

# PRD: Trace-Column Pet

## Problem & Context
Watching a long kitsoki session is a lonely wait at the trace
column — the right-hand observer panel just ticks by. A small
ambient companion adds warmth and life without taking screen space.

## Target Users
Developers watching a long kitsoki session's trace column in the
web UI.

## Goals
- A single small animated SVG pet anchored at the BOTTOM of the
  trace column that idles (bob/blink) while the session runs.
- Opt-in and never in the way — it must never block trace content.

## Requirements
- Functional: an inline-SVG sprite with a CSS keyframe idle
  animation, optionally a subtle reaction when a turn is running.
- Non-functional: pure inline SVG + CSS, no images, no deps;
  headline metric is opt-in-pet-enabled-sessions.

## Open Questions
- Should the pet react when a turn is running, or only idle?
kitsoki writes the validated requirements document.

· Owner: Product · Source idea: "give the trace column a little life"

— the right-hand observer panel in the web drive view that just ticks rows by while the agent thinks, tests run, and a fix lands. There is nothing alive on the screen, and a long agent loop can feel like staring at a log. A small ambient companion can add warmth and life to that wait

Opt-in and never in the way
A full multi-state companion overlay or its own panel (this is one small sprite).
A topbar widget or anything outside the trace column.
Image/sprite-sheet assets, sound, or any third-party animation library.
An inline-SVG sprite rendered at the bottom of the trace column, absolutely
: the pet only mounts when the setting is enabled.
a subtle reaction when a turn is running (e.g. a slightly faster   bob) — driven by state the trace column already has. - An
(a gentle bob and an occasional blink). -
positioned so trace rows are never displaced. - A CSS keyframe
Pure inline SVG + CSS — no images, no new dependencies, no network.
No measurable impact on trace rendering or scroll performance.
With the setting enabled, a small animated pet appears at the bottom of the

trace column and idles continuously. - With the setting disabled (the default), no pet is mounted and nothing changes. - The pet never overlaps or pushes trace rows; the column scrolls normally. - No new asset files, dependencies, or network requests are introduced.
Very short trace column → the pet stays anchored to the column bottom, not the
last row. - Reduced-motion preference → the idle animation is dampened or paused. - Narrow viewport → the pet scales down and never clips the scrollbar.
sprite + dock seam behind the opt-in toggle (off).
Document the setting in the web UI reference.
Announce in the changelog; purely additive, no migration.
